methodology: Articles sourced from Virginia-specific RSS feeds and Google News RSS, filtered for AI/technology/workforce relevance, then assessed by Claude against the scenario framework. Each article is tagged with the region it most affects and the scenario it signals toward.

signals.title: New partnership between VCU and AI Ready RVA aims to boost AI education, innovation and community impact - VCU News
signals.signal_reading: VCU's partnership with AI Ready RVA signals intentional distribution of deep AI literacy beyond NoVA through community-embedded education infrastructure, potentially seeding peer learning networks that could challenge geographic concentration patterns.

signals.title: EPA grant backs community-driven workforce training in Southwest Virginia - Virginia Tech News
signals.signal_reading: EPA-funded community-driven workforce training in Southwest Virginia demonstrates place-based capacity building in historically underserved regions, testing whether deep technical skills can take root outside traditional tech corridors through local ownership models.

signals.title: ‘Shaping our future’: Company powering AI data centers invests $50+ million in Henrico, adding 200 jobs - WWBT
signals.signal_reading: A $50M AI data center infrastructure investment in Henrico concentrates deep technical capability in Richmond Metro, reinforcing the pattern where capital-intensive AI deployment clusters in established metros rather than distributing to rural regions.

signals.title: AI pressure grows on Richmond's young workforce - Axios
signals.signal_reading: AI displacing entry-level tasks that build professional skills threatens to create a shallow adoption pattern where young workers use tools without developing underlying competencies, potentially hollowing out the pathway to deep expertise even in metro areas.

signals.title: RC-RV partners with ed2go to launch innovative, on-demand workforce training programs - Roanoke College
signals.signal_reading: Roanoke College's partnership with ed2go for on-demand workforce training suggests platform-mediated skill delivery that could achieve geographic distribution but risks shallow engagement compared to deeper community-embedded learning models like Virginia Tech's EPA grant program.
